agents.backstory_hint: Systematic analyst skilled at categorizing file changes based on path patterns.
agents.goal: Examine PR diffs to determine ownership areas and identify sensitive file patterns.

agents.backstory_hint: Workflow expert focused on developer velocity and risk mitigation.
agents.goal: Assign PRs to appropriate subject matter experts and flag sensitive PRs for secondary review.

ambiguities: Do you have an existing list of mapping of file paths to team roles, or should the agent derive this from team structure?
Should the system default to assigning to the PR author's team if a file has no clear owner pattern?
assumptions: Files in /auth/ or /payments/ paths are automatically classified as sensitive.
Non-sensitive PRs are assigned to a single reviewer from the general on-call rotation.
Trivial PRs (docs/config) are assigned to any available developer.

tasks.description: Analyze the changed files in the PR, matching them against ownership patterns (/auth/, /payments/) and determining if it qualifies as small/trivial documentation or config changes.
tasks.expected_output: A report identifying the ownership area, sensitivity status (e.g., sensitive vs standard), and change size (trivial vs significant).

tasks.description: Based on the analysis, assign the PR to the relevant subject matter expert or on-call engineer via GitHub API. If sensitive, flag for a second reviewer.
tasks.expected_output: Confirmation of PR assignment to the correct reviewer(s) on GitHub.

tools.name: GitHub API
tools.purpose: Fetch PR diffs and perform review assignments.

workflow.description: Automatically route PRs to the correct subject matter experts based on file changes and flag sensitive changes for mandatory secondary review.
workflow.name: Intelligent PR Routing and Assignment
workflow.trigger: A pull request is opened in GitHub.
